blocked_items.rationale: Topology cannot be generated automatically; user-supplied adjacency is required before runtime use.
blocked_items.scientific_risk: Fabricated adjacency would invalidate legal action constraints and offloading legality.
blocked_items.validation_plan: Confirm user-provided edges match the paper or approved assumptions before any runtime consumer reads them.

blocked_items.rationale: Legal horizontal destinations depend on explicit topology and cannot be inferred safely.
blocked_items.scientific_risk: Guessing destinations would produce invalid routing legality and corrupt offloading decisions.
blocked_items.validation_plan: Validate destinations against a manually approved topology file before enabling runtime use.

blocked_items.rationale: Timeout must be explicitly supplied; inventing a deadline would fabricate a key control parameter.
blocked_items.scientific_risk: An invented timeout would change drop behavior, reward timing, and deadline enforcement.
blocked_items.validation_plan: Validate a manually supplied timeout against paper notes or approved assumptions before runtime use.

entries.rationale: Topology cannot be generated automatically; user-supplied adjacency is required before runtime use.
entries.scientific_risk: Fabricated adjacency would invalidate legal action constraints and offloading legality.
entries.validation_plan: Confirm user-provided edges match the paper or approved assumptions before any runtime consumer reads them.

entries.rationale: Legal horizontal destinations depend on explicit topology and cannot be inferred safely.
entries.scientific_risk: Guessing destinations would produce invalid routing legality and corrupt offloading decisions.
entries.validation_plan: Validate destinations against a manually approved topology file before enabling runtime use.

entries.rationale: Current runtime defaults can be surfaced as an explicit assumption, but the paper did not recover the capacity value.
entries.scientific_risk: A wrong private capacity would distort computation-delay estimates and scheduling feasibility.
entries.validation_plan: Compare simulated delay behavior against any future approved capacity source before enabling runtime use.

entries.rationale: The public/edge capacity is a runtime default only; it is not paper-recovered evidence.
entries.scientific_risk: An incorrect edge capacity would change completion timing and resource contention behavior.
entries.validation_plan: Verify downstream use only after an explicit approval review and a regression comparison against reference traces.

entries.rationale: Cloud capacity is surfaced as a proposed runtime assumption because the paper did not recover a numeric value.
entries.scientific_risk: Overstating cloud capacity would suppress delay and hide contention in offloaded execution paths.
entries.validation_plan: Require explicit user approval and trace-based regression checks before any runtime config consumes the value.

entries.rationale: The cloud-facing rate may reuse the vertical data-rate assumption as an explicit proposal, but it is not paper-backed.
entries.scientific_risk: A wrong transfer rate would distort transmission delay and offload selection behavior.
entries.validation_plan: Compare offload latency traces against the approved rate before runtime use is enabled.

entries.rationale: Timeout must be explicitly supplied; inventing a deadline would fabricate a key control parameter.
entries.scientific_risk: An invented timeout would change drop behavior, reward timing, and deadline enforcement.
entries.validation_plan: Validate a manually supplied timeout against paper notes or approved assumptions before runtime use.

entries.proposed_value: sum rewards per agent per episode, then arithmetic mean across agents
entries.rationale: A transparent reduction rule can be proposed for approval without claiming paper recovery.
entries.scientific_risk: A wrong aggregation order would alter reported rewards and cross-agent comparability.
entries.validation_plan: Confirm the reduction order against downstream reporting artifacts before runtime use is enabled.
